(FKA SCHERING PLOUGH CORPORATION)'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CLAIMS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS HEARING SET FOR JAN-28-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS ORGANON USA, INC., ORGANON INTERNATIONAL INC., MERCK & CO. (FKA SCHERING PLOUGH CORPORATION)'S MOTION TO SEAL CERTAIN DOCUMENTS SUBMITTED IN SUPPORT OF MERCK DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CLAIMS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS IS GRANTED BECAUSE THERE IS NO OPPOSITION FILED AND MOVING PARTY MEETS THE REQUIREMENTS OF CRC 2.550 ET SEQ. THE MOVING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WITH THE FINDINGS REQUIRED BY CRC 2.550(D) AND (E). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANT MCKESSON CORPORATION'S JOINDER IN MERCK DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS - THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO MS. BYRA MARBLE. SHE IS A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM WITH REGARD TO HER CLAIM. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT MCKESSON IS ALSO A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AND STAYS THE ACTION AS TO THE NON-CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS AND SEVERS THEIR CLAIMS. THE TORTS DID NOT OCCUR IN CALIFORNIA, THE COURT CAN REASONABLY ASSUME THAT THE SOURCES OF PROOF AND PHYSICIANS REMAIN IN THE HOME STATES BASED ON THE FACT SHEETS FILED IN FEDERAL COURT, AND CALIFORNIA HAS LITTLE INTEREST IN ADJUDICATING THEIR CLAIMS. THE PRESENCE OF MCKESSON IS LESS COMPELLING AS TO THESE PLAINTIFFS. SEE STANGVIK V. SHILEY, INC., (1991) 54 CAL.3D 744, 756. THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION IS CONDITIONED ON DEFENDANTS AGREEING TO SUBMIT TO JURISDICTION IN THE HOME STATES OF PLAINTIFFS, AND DEFENDANTS CONTINUING TO ABIDE BY THEIR STATEMENT IN THEIR MOVING PAPERS THAT THEY AGREE THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS TOLLED AS OF THE FILING OF THIS LAWSUIT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART MERCK DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CLAIMS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS ORGANON USA, INC., ORGANON INTERNATIONAL INC., ORGANON PHARMACEUTICALS USA INC., LLC (FKA ORGANON PHARMACEUTICALS USA INC.), MERCK & CO. (FKA SCHERING PLOUGH CORPORATION)'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY CLAIMS FOR FORUM NON CONVENIENS - THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION AS TO MS. BYRA MARBLE. SHE IS A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT AND DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT CALIFORNIA IS A SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT FORUM WITH REGARD TO HER CLAIM. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT MCKESSON IS ALSO A CALIFORNIA RESIDENT. THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION AND STAYS THE ACTION AS TO THE NON-CALIFORNIA RESIDENTS AND SEVERS THEIR CLAIMS. THE TORTS DID NOT OCCUR IN CALIFORNIA, THE COURT CAN REASONABLY ASSUME THAT THE SOURCES OF PROOF AND PHYSICIANS REMAIN IN THE HOME STATES BASED ON THE FACT SHEETS FILED IN FEDERAL COURT, AND CALIFORNIA HAS LITTLE INTEREST IN ADJUDICATING THEIR CLAIMS. THE PRESENCE OF MCKESSON IS LESS COMPELLING AS TO THESE PLAINTIFFS. SEE STANGVIK V. SHILEY, INC., (1991) 54 CAL.3D 744, 756. THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION IS CONDITIONED ON DEFENDANTS AGREEING TO SUBMIT TO JURISDICTION IN THE HOME STATES OF PLAINTIFFS, AND DEFENDANTS CONTINUING TO ABIDE BY THEIR STATEMENT IN THEIR MOVING PAPERS THAT THEY AGREE THAT THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS IS TOLLED AS OF THE FILING OF THIS LAWSUIT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling

(Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling

Defendants Organon Usa, Inc., Organon International Inc., Merck & Co. (Fka Schering Plough Corporation)'S Motion To Seal Certain Documents Submitted In Support Of Merck Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Mar-15-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Steven J. Boranian, Esq. of Reed Smith, Tel. 415.659.5980, for defendants and moving party Organon and Merck;
Wendi J. Frisch, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy, Tel. 213.891.9100, for defendant McKeeson Corporation by Court Call;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. Defendants Organon Usa, Inc., Organon International Inc., Merck & Co. (Fka Schering Plough Corporation)'s Motion To Seal Certain Documents Submitted In Support Of Merck Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims For Forum Non Conveniens is granted because there is no opposition filed and moving party meets the requirements of CRC 2.550 et seq. The moving party is to prepare a proposed order with the findings required by CRC 2.550(d) and (e). Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Defendant Mckesson Corporation'S Joinder In Merck Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
Off Calendar Mar-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Wendi J. Frisch, Esq. of Morris Polich & Purdy, Tel. 213.891.9100, for defendant and moving party McKeeson Corporation by Court Call;
Steven J. Boranian, Esq. of Reed Smith, Tel. 415.659.5980, for defendants Organon and Merck;
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. Defendant Mckesson Corporation'S Joinder In Merck Defendants' Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens - The Court denies the motion as to Ms. Byra Marble. She is a California resident and Defendant fails to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum with regard to her claim. The Court notes that Defendant McKesson is also a California resident. The Court grants the motion and stays the action as to the non-California residents and severs their claims. The torts did not occur in California, the Court can reasonably assume that the sources of proof and physicians remain in the home states based on the Fact Sheets filed in federal court, and California has little interest in adjudicating their claims. The presence of McKesson is less compelling as to these plaintiffs. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc., (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 756. The granting of the motion is conditioned on defendants agreeing to submit to jurisdiction in the home states of plaintiffs, and defendants continuing to abide by their statement in their moving papers that they agree that the statute of limitations is tolled as of the filing of this lawsuit. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

She is a California resident and Defendant fails to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum with regard to her claim. The Court notes that Defendant McKesson is also a California resident. The Court grants the motion and stays the action as to the non-California residents and severs their claims. The torts did not occur in California, the Court can reasonably assume that the sources of proof and physicians remain in the home states based on the Fact Sheets filed in federal court, and California has little interest in adjudicating their claims. The presence of McKesson is less compelling as to these plaintiffs. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc., (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 756. The granting of the motion is conditioned on defendants agreeing to submit to jurisdiction in the home states of plaintiffs, and defendants continuing to abide by their statement in their moving papers that they agree that the statute of limitations is tolled as of the filing of this lawsuit. MINI MINUTES:
